currentState: Old familiarity forced into crisis partnership. Chapter 21 lets Muradin trust Jaina fast because she can name what Frostmourne is, and lets Jaina trust Muradin because he brings the local truth without performance.
powerDynamic: Muradin supplies terrain, timing, and personal knowledge of Arthas. Jaina supplies the magical frame that explains why Frostmourne belongs inside the plague war at all. Their authority is complementary rather than competitive.

physicalDynamics.pattern: The chapter places them across Muradin's table first, with Jaina translating magical horror into language blunt enough for him to use at once.
By the retreat Muradin has become weight on a carry line and Jaina has become one of the people refusing to let that weight be surrendered.

nameUsage.jainaCallsMuradin: Uses Muradin with old familiarity and no court distance.
nameUsage.muradinCallsJaina: Starts with Lady, then settles into practical peer speech once the work begins.

history.event: Muradin receives Jaina as someone from Arthas's old world and very quickly shifts to relying on her present competence instead of shared past alone.
Jaina identifies Frostmourne as plague-kin in front of Muradin, giving his curse warning a larger frame and making them immediate working allies.
The cave fight ends with Jaina helping carry Muradin out alive, converting briefing-room alignment into bodily rescue obligation.

trajectory: Old familiarity renewed under crisis -> chapter 21's fast, practical alliance around naming and surviving Frostmourne.
